column4: Electrical generation and distribution systems using alternating current for efficient voltage transformation and long-distance transmission.
Electromagnetic devices that step AC voltage up or down for efficient transmission, distribution, and isolation.
Rotary turbine generators that convert high-pressure steam into large-scale electric power.
Power stations that convert falling or flowing water into electricity through turbines and generators.
Electrical transmission lines and substations that move bulk power over long distances at high voltage.
Solid-state circuits that convert, switch, and control electric power for drives, grids, renewables, and vehicles.
Power plants that combine gas turbines and steam cycles to improve fuel efficiency and operational flexibility.
Electric grids with sensors, communications, automation, and analytics for monitoring, control, reliability, and distributed resources.
Grid-scale storage that pumps water uphill when power is cheap and releases it through turbines when power is needed.
Large photovoltaic plants with inverters, trackers, controls, and grid interconnection for bulk electricity generation.
Converters and controls that condition variable wind-turbine output for reliable grid integration.
Grid storage systems designed to discharge over many hours, days, or seasons to support high-renewable electricity systems.
Rocket engines using pumped or pressurized liquid propellants for controllable high-performance thrust.
Multi-stage rockets capable of accelerating payloads to orbital velocity and deploying spacecraft.
Sensors, actuators, computers, and algorithms that determine and control spacecraft position, attitude, and trajectory.
Artificial satellites that relay radio, television, telephone, internet, and data signals across large regions.
Orbital platforms carrying optical, radar, thermal, or multispectral sensors for mapping, weather, climate, and security.
Orbital observatories that place telescopes above atmospheric distortion and absorption for astronomical observations.
Launch systems designed to recover and refly major stages or spacecraft to reduce launch cost and cadence limits.
Standardized small spacecraft that lower satellite development cost and enable rapid educational, commercial, and scientific missions.
Coordinated groups of satellites providing coverage, revisit rate, communications capacity, or navigation performance beyond one spacecraft.
Spacecraft operations that inspect, refuel, repair, upgrade, tow, or assemble satellites and structures in orbit.
Closed-loop actuator systems that use feedback to control position, speed, torque, or force precisely.
Programmable mechanical arms and end effectors for positioning, grasping, welding, assembly, and handling tasks.
Robotic manipulators deployed in factories for welding, painting, assembly, machine tending, and material handling.
Camera, lighting, and image-processing systems that let robots inspect objects, locate parts, and guide actions.
Robotic systems that plan and execute motion through environments using sensing, localization, maps, and control.
Simultaneous localization and mapping methods that let robots build maps while estimating their own position.
Robots designed to work near people with force limits, sensing, safety controls, and easier programming.
Robotic systems that assist minimally invasive surgery through precise instruments, teleoperation, imaging, and motion scaling.
Mobile robots that move inventory, carts, or shelves through warehouses using navigation, fleet control, and integration with logistics software.
Autonomous flight control, perception, planning, and fleet operations for uncrewed aerial vehicles.
Mobile manipulation robots designed to perform varied human-environment tasks using dexterous hardware and foundation-model control.
Robotic learning systems constrained by formal checks, simulation, runtime monitors, and safety cases before acting in the physical world.
